nativeRange.habitat: Found on protected coral and rubble slopes throughout Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines, typically at depths of 6 to 40 m, where it tends to associate with finely branching corals.

diet.notes: Tends to accept a varied diet of meaty frozen foods such as vitamin-enriched mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, krill, and finely chopped seafood. Has been reported to take high-quality marine flake and pellet foods as well.

avoidWith: other male fairy wrasses in small tanks

summary: A peaceful reef wrasse from the Western Pacific, generally considered reef-safe and notable for the bold color variation displayed by males. Well suited to community tanks, though a tight-fitting lid is essential as this species tends to jump.
careNotes: The Yellow Fin Fairy Wrasse is generally considered reef-safe and has been reported to leave corals and invertebrates alone. Males tend to display highly variable coloration — the dorsal fin can range from yellow to red or blue, and body coloration from red, pink, or white — with changes tied to mood or maturity. Females are typically less colorful and less commonly available commercially. The species tends to form loose aggregations in the wild and may be kept as a pair or small group in larger tanks, though conspecific aggression has been noted in confined spaces. A tight-fitting canopy is advisable, as these fish have been documented jumping from open aquariums. A shaded area with live rock is recommended for retreat. The species is documented as a protogynous hermaphrodite (female-first sex changer) and tends to form harems of one dominant male and multiple females in the wild.
breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity has been documented but is considered difficult. The species is a protogynous hermaphrodite — individuals begin as females and the dominant individual can transition to a male. Distinct pairing behavior has been observed during spawning events in the wild.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size, temperament, diet, reef safety, and care level. BlueZoo listed 30 gal minimum tank vs LiveAquaria's 60 gal; adult size given as 2.75 in vs LiveAquaria's 3 in.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (6.5 cm TL), native range (Eastern Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines), habitat depth (6–40 m), and wild temperature (28–29°C). FishBase temperature is higher than trade-source recommendations; averaged in final temperatureC range.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (3–4 in), minimum tank size (40 gal), water parameters (74–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025), care level (moderate), temperament (peaceful), and reef safety (yes). Tank size disagreement: 40 gal vs LiveAquaria 60 gal vs BlueZoo 30 gal; biased toward larger (60 gal / 250 L) per playbook rule for active swimmers.

fish.breedingNotes: The Yellow Fin Fairy Wrasse is a protogynous hermaphrodite, meaning individuals begin life as females and the dominant fish in a group can transition to male. Captive spawning has been documented, but rearing larvae through to settlement is rarely achieved. Females can also transition to male when the dominant male is absent.
